title: Gut-Brain Connection Self-Assessment
description: Evaluate your current understanding of the gut-brain connection and how your dietary and lifestyle habits may be affecting your mental health through your gut.
instructions: Over the past 2 weeks, how often have you experienced the following?

questions.text: Digestive discomfort (bloating, gas, irregular bowel movements)
Feeling that your mood drops after eating certain foods
Energy crashes or brain fog after meals
Cravings for sugary or highly processed foods
Difficulty concentrating or mental fatigue unrelated to sleep
Anxiety or low mood that seems connected to what you ate
Skin issues, headaches, or other inflammatory symptoms
Feeling that stress directly affects your digestion
Using food to cope with difficult emotions
Feeling disconnected from hunger and fullness signals

bands.label: Minimal Gut-Brain Concerns
bands.description: Your responses suggest your gut-brain connection is functioning well. You experience few symptoms linking digestion, diet, and mood. Continue your current healthy habits and use this course to deepen your understanding of the science behind what you're already doing right.

bands.label: Mild Gut-Brain Concerns
bands.description: You're experiencing some symptoms that may reflect a gut-brain connection worth exploring. Research shows that dietary patterns can influence mood through inflammation, blood sugar, and the microbiome. This course will help you identify specific changes that could make a difference.

bands.label: Moderate Gut-Brain Concerns
bands.description: Your responses suggest a noticeable connection between your digestive health, diet, and mood. Studies show that gut inflammation and microbiome imbalances can significantly affect mental health. This course will help you understand these pathways and develop practical strategies for improvement.

bands.label: Significant Gut-Brain Concerns
bands.description: You're experiencing frequent symptoms that suggest your gut-brain connection may benefit from attention. Consider discussing these symptoms with a healthcare provider who understands nutritional psychiatry. This course will give you the knowledge to have informed conversations with your care team.

disclaimer: This is an educational self-check tool, not a clinical diagnosis. It is designed to help you reflect on potential connections between your gut health and mood. If you are experiencing significant digestive or mental health symptoms, please consult a healthcare provider. If you are in crisis, call or text 988.
